context: Evaluates whether the candidate correctly implements the HITL-first pagination pattern for large CVE dataset retrieval. The critical requirement is that the pagination strategy prompt must be the very first user-visible action, before any tool calls or identifier resolution.
capability_tested: Pagination Handling for Large Datasets with HITL

checklist.name: HITL pagination prompt is first action
checklist.description: The pagination prompt is the very first user-visible response. No MCP tool calls (not find_host_by_name, not get_system_cves, not any other tool) appear before this prompt in the workflow. The workflow explicitly states 'WAIT for user response. Do NOT proceed until response received.'

checklist.name: Three pagination options offered
checklist.description: The pagination prompt offers at least three distinct options: (1) first page only (quick, may miss results), (2) all pages (recommended for remediatable), and (3) N pages (user-specified count). Each option includes a brief description of its tradeoff.

checklist.name: First-page limitation warning
checklist.description: The pagination prompt includes an explicit warning that first-page-only results may return 0 remediatable CVEs because remediatable vulnerabilities may appear on any page. The 'all pages' option is marked as recommended for remediatable queries.

checklist.name: Correct pagination loop
checklist.description: After strategy selection, the implementation uses limit=100 per page request and increments the offset (or page number) on each iteration. The loop correctly stops when: response count < limit (last page), user-specified page count reached, or no more results.

checklist.name: UUID resolution after HITL
checklist.description: The hostname-to-UUID resolution (inventory lookup) occurs only after the user has responded to the pagination prompt, not before. The workflow order is: HITL prompt → await response → resolve hostname → paginate.
